In v1beta1 this block holds","description":"without a name-preserving graduation path. In v1beta1 this block holds","depth":1,"path":"spec.experimental","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":199,"text":"  # `gpuMemoryService` and `failover` (which remain tightly coupled -- failover","description":"`gpuMemoryService` and `failover` (which remain tightly coupled -- failover","depth":1,"path":"spec.experimental","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":200,"text":"  # requires GMS -- and are expected to evolve together as the DRA-based GPU","description":"requires GMS -- and are expected to evolve together as the DRA-based GPU","depth":1,"path":"spec.experimental","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":201,"text":"  # sharing story matures), and `checkpoint` (whose interaction with the","description":"sharing story matures), and `checkpoint` (whose interaction with the","depth":1,"path":"spec.experimental","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":202,"text":"  # standalone DynamoCheckpoint resource and identity-hash computation is still","description":"standalone DynamoCheckpoint resource and identity-hash computation is still","depth":1,"path":"spec.experimental","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":203,"text":"  # settling). Fields here are explicitly NOT covered by the normal v1beta1","description":"settling). Fields here are explicitly NOT covered by the normal v1beta1","depth":1,"path":"spec.experimental","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":204,"text":"  # deprecation policy; do not depend on them for production workloads.","description":"deprecation policy; do not depend on them for production workloads.","depth":1,"path":"spec.experimental","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":205,"text":"  experimental: # optional","description":"experimental groups opt-in preview features whose API shape and\nbehavior may change in breaking ways between v1beta1 releases,\nincluding disappearing without a name-preserving graduation path.\nIn v1beta1 this block holds `gpuMemoryService` and `failover` (which\nremain tightly coupled -- failover requires GMS -- and are expected to\nevolve together as the DRA-based GPU sharing story matures), and\n`checkpoint` (whose interaction with the standalone DynamoCheckpoint\nresource and identity-hash computation is still settling). Fields here\nare explicitly NOT covered by the normal v1beta1 deprecation policy;\ndo not depend on them for production workloads.","depth":1,"field":"experimental","path":"spec.experimental","code":true,"foldable":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental"},{"index":206,"text":"    # checkpoint configures container-image snapshotting and restore for this","description":"checkpoint configures container-image snapshotting and restore for this","depth":2,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":207,"text":"    # component. When set, the DGD controller can produce a DynamoCheckpoint CR","depth":2,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":208,"text":"    # from a running pod and later restore pods from that checkpoint for faster","description":"from a running pod and later restore pods from that checkpoint for faster","depth":2,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":209,"text":"    # cold start. The user-facing shape of this field -- especially its","description":"cold start. The user-facing shape of this field -- especially its","depth":2,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":210,"text":"    # interaction with the standalone DynamoCheckpoint resource and the","description":"interaction with the standalone DynamoCheckpoint resource and the","depth":2,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":211,"text":"    # identity-hash computation -- is still settling, which is why it lives","description":"identity-hash computation -- is still settling, which is why it lives","depth":2,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":212,"text":"    # under `experimental` in v1beta1 instead of at the top level.","description":"under `experimental` in v1beta1 instead of at the top level.","depth":2,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":213,"text":"    checkpoint: # optional","description":"checkpoint configures container-image snapshotting and restore for\nthis component. When set, the DGD controller can produce a\nDynamoCheckpoint CR from a running pod and later restore pods from\nthat checkpoint for faster cold start. The user-facing shape of this\nfield -- especially its interaction with the standalone\nDynamoCheckpoint resource and the identity-hash computation -- is\nstill settling, which is why it lives under `experimental` in v1beta1\ninstead of at the top level.","depth":2,"field":"checkpoint","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint"},{"index":214,"text":"      # checkpointRef references an existing DynamoCheckpoint CR by","description":"checkpointRef references an existing DynamoCheckpoint CR by","depth":3,"path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.checkpointRef","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-checkpointref"},{"index":215,"text":"      # `metadata.name`. The value must\nmatch the `name` of a container in that list; the operator merges its\nfrontend-sidecar defaults (auto-generated Dynamo env vars, ports,\nhealth probes) into that container the same way it merges into `\"main\"`.\nThe full container definition (image, args, envFrom, env) lives in\n`podTemplate` -- this eliminates the redundant `image`, `args`,\n`envFromSecret`, and `envs` fields from v1alpha1's `FrontendSidecarSpec`.\nThe validation webhook rejects values that do not match any container\nname in `podTemplate.spec.containers`.","depth":1,"field":"frontendSidecar","path":"spec.frontendSidecar","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-frontendsidecar"},{"index":324,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-324"},{"index":325,"text":"  # globalDynamoNamespace places the component in the global Dynamo namespace","description":"globalDynamoNamespace places the component in the global Dynamo namespace","depth":1,"path":"spec.globalDynamoNamespace","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-globaldynamonamespace"},{"index":326,"text":"  # rather than the per-deployment namespace derived from the DGD name.","description":"rather than the per-deployment namespace derived from the DGD name.","depth":1,"path":"spec.globalDynamoNamespace","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-globaldynamonamespace"},{"index":327,"text":"  # globalDynamoNamespace: <boolean>","description":"globalDynamoNamespace places the component in the global Dynamo\nnamespace rather than the per-deployment namespace derived from the\nDGD name.","depth":1,"field":"globalDynamoNamespace","path":"spec.globalDynamoNamespace","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-globaldynamonamespace"},{"index":328,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-328"},{"index":329,"text":"  # modelRef references a model served by this component. `name` is decoupled from the underlying Kubernetes resource name so that the","description":"`name` is decoupled from the underlying Kubernetes resource name so that the","depth":1,"path":"spec.name","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-name"},{"index":353,"text":"  # operator can rename child workloads (e.g. suffixing worker DCDs with a hash","description":"operator can rename child workloads (e.g. suffixing worker DCDs with a hash","depth":1,"path":"spec.name","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-name"},{"index":354,"text":"  # during rolling updates) without losing the stable identity that downstream","description":"during rolling updates) without losing the stable identity that downstream","depth":1,"path":"spec.name","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-name"},{"index":355,"text":"  # depend on.","description":"depend on.","depth":1,"path":"spec.name","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-name"},{"index":357,"text":"  # name: \"<string>\" # minLength: 1, maxLength: 63","description":"name is the stable logical identifier for this component within its\nDynamoGraphDeployment. It must be unique within the parent's\n`spec.components` list.\n\nFor standalone DynamoComponentDeployment objects, the defaulting webhook\npopulates `name` from `metadata.name` on admission, so users\ntypically do not need to set it explicitly.\n\n`name` is decoupled from the underlying Kubernetes resource name so that\nthe operator can rename child workloads (e.g. suffixing worker DCDs with\na hash during rolling updates) without losing the stable identity that\ndownstream consumers (labels, status maps, DGDSA references, planner\nRBAC, EPP filters) depend on.","depth":1,"field":"name","path":"spec.name","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-name"},{"index":358,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-358"},{"index":359,"text":"  # podTemplate is the pod template used to create the component's pods. If no","description":"`podTemplate.spec.containers`, merging user overrides by name. If no","depth":1,"path":"spec.podTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":363,"text":"  # container named `\"main\"` is present, the operator auto-generates it with","description":"container named `\"main\"` is present, the operator auto-generates it with","depth":1,"path":"spec.podTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":364,"text":"  # standard defaults. All other containers in `podTemplate.spec.containers` are","description":"standard defaults. All other containers in `podTemplate.spec.containers` are","depth":1,"path":"spec.podTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":365,"text":"  # treated as user-managed sidecars: the operator does not inject defaults into","description":"treated as user-managed sidecars: the operator does not inject defaults into","depth":1,"path":"spec.podTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":366,"text":"  # them, so sidecars must specify required fields (e.g. `image`) themselves.","description":"them, so sidecars must specify required fields (e.g. `image`) themselves.","depth":1,"path":"spec.podTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":367,"text":"  # The validation webhook rejects pod templates where a non-`\"main\"` container","description":"The validation webhook rejects pod templates where a non-`\"main\"` container","depth":1,"path":"spec.podTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":368,"text":"  # is missing a required field such as `image`.","description":"is missing a required field such as `image`.","depth":1,"path":"spec.podTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":369,"text":"  podTemplate: # optional","description":"podTemplate is the pod template used to create the component's pods.\nThe operator injects its defaults (image, command, env, ports, probes,\nresources, volume mounts) into the container named `\"main\"` inside\n`podTemplate.spec.containers`, merging user overrides by name. If no\ncontainer named `\"main\"` is present, the operator auto-generates it\nwith standard defaults. All other containers in `podTemplate.spec.containers`\nare treated as user-managed sidecars: the operator does not inject\ndefaults into them, so sidecars must specify required fields (e.g. `image`)\nthemselves. The validation webhook rejects pod templates where a\nnon-`\"main\"` container is missing a required field such as `image`.","depth":1,"field":"podTemplate","path":"spec.podTemplate","code":true,"foldable":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate"},{"index":370,"text":"    # Standard object's metadata. Not executed within a shell.\nThe container image's ENTRYPOINT is used if this is not provided.\nVariable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's environment. If a variable\ncannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced\nto a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. \"$$(VAR_NAME)\" will\nproduce the string literal \"$(VAR_NAME)\". Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":819,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-819"},{"index":820,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":952,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-952"},{"index":953,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted.","depth":5,"field":"restartPolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicy"},{"index":1020,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1020"},{"index":1021,"text":"          # Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":1022,"text":"          # container should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":1308,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1308"},{"index":1309,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false","depth":5,"field":"stdinOnce","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].stdinOnce","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-stdinonce"},{"index":1329,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1329"},{"index":1330,"text":"          # Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's","description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":1331,"text":"          # termination message will be written is mounted into the container's","description":"termination message will be written is mounted into the container's","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":1332,"text":"          # filesystem. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":7,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":1405,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1405"},{"index":1406,"text":"              # Path within the volume from which the container's volume should","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should","depth":7,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":1407,"text":"              # be mounted. optional, listType:","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":5,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1442,"text":"                                                           # atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","metadata":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1443,"text":"            - optional, listType:","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":5,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1544,"text":"                                                           # atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. optional, listType:","description":"If the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at\nscheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.\nIf the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met\nat some point during pod execution (e.g. due to a pod label update), the\nsystem may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.\nWhen there are multiple elements, the lists of nodes corresponding to each\npodAffinityTerm are intersected, i.e. all terms must be satisfied.","depth":5,"field":"requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-requiredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1910,"text":"                                                          # More info:","depth":6,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":2325,"text":"            # https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","description":"https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":6,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":2326,"text":"            preStop: # optional","description":"PreStop is called immediately before a container is terminated due to an\nAPI request or management event such as liveness/startup probe failure,\npreemption, resource contention, etc. The handler is not called if the\ncontainer crashes or exits. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":6,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":2327,"text":"              # Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2487,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2487"},{"index":2488,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2611,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2611"},{"index":2612,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2939,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2939"},{"index":2940,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false","depth":5,"field":"stdinOnce","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].stdinOnce","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-stdinonce"},{"index":2960,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2960"},{"index":2961,"text":"          # If set, the name of the container from PodSpec that this ephemeral","description":"If set, the name of the container from PodSpec that this ephemeral","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].targetContainerName","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-targetcontainername"},{"index":2962,"text":"          # container targets. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":7,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":3046,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3046"},{"index":3047,"text":"              # Path within the volume from which the container's volume should","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should","depth":7,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":3048,"text":"              # be mounted. This field is alpha-level and is only honored by servers that","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.hostUsers","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-hostusers"},{"index":3095,"text":"      # enable the UserNamespacesSupport feature.","description":"enable the UserNamespacesSupport feature.","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.hostUsers","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-hostusers"},{"index":3096,"text":"      # hostUsers: <boolean>","description":"Use the host's user namespace.\nOptional: Default to true.\nIf set to true or not present, the pod will be run in the host user namespace, useful\nfor when the pod needs a feature only available to the host user namespace, such as\nloading a kernel module with CAP_SYS_MODULE.\nWhen set to false, a new userns is created for the pod. Setting false is useful for\nmitigating container breakout vulnerabilities even allowing users to run their\ncontainers as root without actually having root privileges on the host.\nThis field is alpha-level and is only honored by servers that enable the UserNamespacesSupport feature.","depth":3,"field":"hostUsers","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.hostUsers","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-hostusers"},{"index":3097,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3097"},{"index":3098,"text":"      # Specifies the hostname of the Pod If not specified, the pod's hostname","description":"Specifies the hostname of the Pod If not specified, the pod's hostname","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.hostname","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-hostname"},{"index":3099,"text":"      # will be set to a system-defined value.","description":"will be set to a system-defined value.","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.hostname","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-hostname"},{"index":3100,"text":"      # hostname: \"<string>\"","description":"Specifies the hostname of the Pod\nIf not specified, the pod's hostname will be set to a system-defined value.","depth":3,"field":"hostname","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.hostname","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-hostname"},{"index":3101,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3101"},{"index":3102,"text":"      # HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname","description":"HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.hostnameOverride","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-hostnameoverride"},{"index":3103,"text":"      # as perceived by the pod. If any\ninit container fails, the pod is considered to have failed and is handled according\nto its restartPolicy. The name for an init container or normal container must be\nunique among all containers.\nInit containers may not have Lifecycle actions, Readiness probes, Liveness probes, or Startup probes.\nThe resourceRequirements of an init container are taken into account during scheduling\nby finding the highest request/limit for each resource type, and then using the max of\nthat value or the sum of the normal containers. Limits are applied to init containers\nin a similar fashion.\nInit containers cannot currently be added or removed.\nCannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/init-containers/","depth":3,"field":"initContainers","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers"},{"index":3141,"text":"        - # Name of the container specified as a DNS_LABEL. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":6,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":3406,"text":"              # Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":7,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":3407,"text":"              # exec:","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":7,"field":"exec","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":3408,"text":"                # Command is the command line to execute inside the container,","description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container,","depth":8,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":3409,"text":"                # the working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","description":"the working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","depth":8,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":3410,"text":"                # container's filesystem. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":3568,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3568"},{"index":3569,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":3701,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3701"},{"index":3702,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted.","depth":5,"field":"restartPolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicy"},{"index":3769,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3769"},{"index":3770,"text":"          # Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicyRules","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":3771,"text":"          # container should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Constraints on the rules:\n- At most 20 rules are allowed.\n- Rules can have the same action.\n- Identical rules are not forbidden in validations.\nWhen rules are specified, container MUST set RestartPolicy explicitly\neven it if matches the Pod's RestartPolicy.","depth":5,"field":"restartPolicyRules","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicyRules","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":3781,"text":"            - # Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the","description":"Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the","depth":7,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicyRules[].action","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicyrules-action"},{"index":3782,"text":"              # requirements are satisfied. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":6,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":4057,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4057"},{"index":4058,"text":"            # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false","depth":5,"field":"stdinOnce","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].stdinOnce","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-stdinonce"},{"index":4078,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4078"},{"index":4079,"text":"          # Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's","description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":4080,"text":"          # termination message will be written is mounted into the container's","description":"termination message will be written is mounted into the container's","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":4081,"text":"          # filesystem. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":7,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":4154,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4154"},{"index":4155,"text":"              # Path within the volume from which the container's volume should","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should","depth":7,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":4156,"text":"              # be mounted. optional","description":"Specifies the OS of the containers in the pod.\nSome pod and container fields are restricted if this is set.\n\nIf the OS field is set to linux, the following fields must be unset:\n-securityContext.windowsOptions\n\nIf the OS field is set to windows, following fields must be unset:\n- spec.hostPID\n- spec.hostIPC\n- spec.hostUsers\n- spec.resources\n- spec.securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy\n- spec.securityContext.sysctls\n- spec.shareProcessNamespace\n- spec.securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.securityContext.runAsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroups\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroupsPolicy\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.procMount\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsGroup","depth":3,"field":"os","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.os","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-os"},{"index":4213,"text":"        # If\nthe RuntimeClass admission controller is enabled, overhead must not be set in Pod create requests.\nThe RuntimeClass admission controller will reject Pod create requests which have the overhead already\nset. If RuntimeClass is configured and selected in the PodSpec, Overhead will be set to the value\ndefined in the corresponding RuntimeClass, otherwise it will remain unset and treated as zero.\nMore info: https://git.k8s.io/enhancements/keps/sig-node/688-pod-overhead/README.md","depth":3,"field":"overhead","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.overhead","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-overhead"},{"index":4232,"text":"        # <key>: <int-or-string> # intOrString","depth":4,"field":"<key>","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.overhead.<key>","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-overhead-key"},{"index":4233,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4233"},{"index":4234,"text":"      # PreemptionPolicy is the Policy for preempting pods with lower priority.","description":"PreemptionPolicy is the Policy for preempting pods with lower priority.","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.preemptionPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-preemptionpolicy"},{"index":4235,"text":"      # One of Never, PreemptLowerPriority. resourceClaimTemplateName: \"<string>\"","description":"ResourceClaimTemplateName is the name of a ResourceClaimTemplate\nobject in the same namespace as this pod.\n\nThe template will be used to create a new ResourceClaim, which will\nbe bound to this pod. When this pod is deleted, the ResourceClaim\nwill also be deleted. The pod name and resource name, along with a\ngenerated component, will be used to form a unique name for the\nResourceClaim, which will be recorded in pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses.\n\nThis field is immutable and no changes will be made to the\ncorresponding ResourceClaim by the control plane after creating the\nResourceClaim.\n\nExactly one of ResourceClaimName and ResourceClaimTemplateName must\nbe set.","depth":5,"field":"resourceClaimTemplateName","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.resourceClaims[].resourceClaimTemplateName","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-resourceclaims-resourceclaimtemplatename"},{"index":4300,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4300"},{"index":4301,"text":"      # Resources is the total amount of CPU and Memory resources required by","description":"Resources is the total amount of CPU and Memory resources required by","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.resources","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-resources"},{"index":4302,"text":"      # all containers in the pod. Note that this field cannot be set when","depth":4,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":4470,"text":"        # spec.os.name is windows.","description":"spec.os.name is windows.","depth":4,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":4471,"text":"        # seLinuxChangePolicy: \"<string>\"","description":"seLinuxChangePolicy defines how the container's SELinux label is applied to all volumes used by the Pod.\nIt has no effect on nodes that do not support SELinux or to volumes does not support SELinux.\nValid values are \"MountOption\" and \"Recursive\".\n\n\"Recursive\" means relabeling of all files on all Pod volumes by the container runtime.\nThis may be slow for large volumes, but allows mixing privileged and unprivileged Pods sharing the same volume on the same node.\n\n\"MountOption\" mounts all eligible Pod volumes with `-o context` mount option.\nThis requires all Pods that share the same volume to use the same SELinux label.\nIt is not possible to share the same volume among privileged and unprivileged Pods.\nEligible volumes are in-tree FibreChannel and iSCSI volumes, and all CSI volumes\nwhose CSI driver announces SELinux support by setting spec.seLinuxMount: true in their\nCSIDriver instance. Other volumes are always re-labelled recursively.\n\"MountOption\" value is allowed only when SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled.\n\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled, \"MountOption\" is used.\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is disabled, \"MountOption\" is used for ReadWriteOncePod volumes\nand \"Recursive\" for all other volumes.\n\nThis field affects only Pods that have SELinux label set, either in PodSecurityContext or in SecurityContext of all containers.\n\nAll Pods that use the same volume should use the same seLinuxChangePolicy, otherwise some pods can get stuck in ContainerCreating state.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","depth":4,"field":"seLinuxChangePolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":4472,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4472"},{"index":4473,"text":"        # The SELinux context to be applied to all containers. setHostnameAsFQDN: <boolean>","description":"If true the pod's hostname will be configured as the pod's FQDN, rather than the leaf name (the default).\nIn Linux containers, this means setting the FQDN in the hostname field of the kernel (the nodename field of struct utsname).\nIn Windows containers, this means setting the registry value of hostname for the registry key HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\\\SYSTEM\\\\CurrentControlSet\\\\Services\\\\Tcpip\\\\Parameters to FQDN.\nIf a pod does not have FQDN, this has no effect.\nDefault to false.","depth":3,"field":"setHostnameAsFQDN","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.setHostnameAsFQDN","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-sethostnameasfqdn"},{"index":4588,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4588"},{"index":4589,"text":"      # Optional: Default to false.","description":"cannot both be set. Optional: Default to false.","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.shareProcessNamespace","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-shareprocessnamespace"},{"index":4594,"text":"      # shareProcessNamespace: <boolean>","description":"Share a single process namespace between all of the containers in a pod.\nWhen this is set containers will be able to view and signal processes from other containers\nin the same pod, and the first process in each container will not be assigned PID 1.\nHostPID and ShareProcessNamespace cannot both be set.\nOptional: Default to false.","depth":3,"field":"shareProcessNamespace","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.shareProcessNamespace","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-shareprocessnamespace"},{"index":4595,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4595"},{"index":4596,"text":"      # If specified, the fully qualified Pod hostname will be","description":"If specified, the fully qualified Pod hostname will be","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.subdomain","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-subdomain"},{"index":4597,"text":"      # \"<hostname>.<subdomain>.<pod namespace>.svc.<cluster domain>\". May be decreased in delete request.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nIf this value is nil, the default grace period will be used instead.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nDefaults to 30 seconds.","depth":3,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":4611,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4611"},{"index":4612,"text":"      # If specified, the pod's tolerations.","description":"If specified, the pod's tolerations.","depth":3,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.tolerations","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-tolerations"},{"index":4613,"text":"      # tolerations: # listType: atomic","description":"If specified, the pod's tolerations.","depth":3,"field":"tolerations","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.tolerations","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-tolerations"},{"index":4614,"text":"        # - # Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Exists is","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.tolerations[].operator","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-tolerations-operator"},{"index":4626,"text":"          # equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all","description":"equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.tolerations[].operator","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-tolerations-operator"},{"index":4627,"text":"          # taints of a particular category.","description":"taints of a particular category.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.tolerations[].operator","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-tolerations-operator"},{"index":4628,"text":"          # operator: \"<string>\"","description":"Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\nValid operators are Exists and Equal. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","description":"It's a required field. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":4662,"text":"          maxSkew: <int32> # required","description":"MaxSkew describes the degree to which pods may be unevenly distributed.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=DoNotSchedule`, it is the maximum permitted difference\nbetween the number of matching pods in the target topology and the global minimum.\nThe global minimum is the minimum number of matching pods in an eligible domain\nor zero if the number of eligible domains is less than MinDomains.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/1:\nIn this case, the global minimum is 1.\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |   P   |\n- if MaxSkew is 1, incoming pod can only be scheduled to zone3 to become 2/2/2;\nscheduling it onto zone1(zone2) would make the ActualSkew(3-1) on zone1(zone2)\nviolate MaxSkew(1).\n- if MaxSkew is 2, incoming pod can be scheduled onto any zone.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway`, it is used to give higher precedence\nto topologies that satisfy it.\nIt's a required field. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","depth":5,"field":"maxSkew","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":4663,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4663"},{"index":4664,"text":"          # TopologyKey is the key of node labels. It's a required field.","description":"domain of that topology. It's a required field.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":4674,"text":"          topologyKey: \"<string>\" # required","description":"TopologyKey is the key of node labels. Nodes that have a label with this key\nand identical values are considered to be in the same topology.\nWe consider each <key, value> as a \"bucket\", and try to put balanced number\nof pods into each bucket.\nWe define a domain as a particular instance of a topology.\nAlso, we define an eligible domain as a domain whose nodes meet the requirements of\nnodeAffinityPolicy and nodeTaintsPolicy.\ne.g. If TopologyKey is \"kubernetes.io/hostname\", each Node is a domain of that topology.\nAnd, if TopologyKey is \"topology.kubernetes.io/zone\", each zone is a domain of that topology.\nIt's a required field.","depth":5,"field":"topologyKey","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":4675,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4675"},{"index":4676,"text":"          # WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't","description":"WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4677,"text":"          # satisfy the spread constraint. - For example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4684,"text":"          # 1, and pods with the same labelSelector spread as 3/1/1: | zone1 |","description":"1, and pods with the same labelSelector spread as 3/1/1: | zone1 |","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4685,"text":"          # zone2 | zone3 | | P P P | P | P | If WhenUnsatisfiable is set to","description":"zone2 | zone3 | | P P P | P | P | If WhenUnsatisfiable is set to","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4686,"text":"          # DoNotSchedule, incoming pod can only be scheduled to zone2(zone3) to","description":"DoNotSchedule, incoming pod can only be scheduled to zone2(zone3) to","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4687,"text":"          # become 3/2/1(3/1/2) as ActualSkew(2-1) on zone2(zone3) satisfies","description":"become 3/2/1(3/1/2) as ActualSkew(2-1) on zone2(zone3) satisfies","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4688,"text":"          # MaxSkew(1). In other words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but","description":"MaxSkew(1). In other words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4689,"text":"          # scheduler won't make it *more* imbalanced. It's a required field.","description":"scheduler won't make it *more* imbalanced. It's a required field.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4690,"text":"          whenUnsatisfiable: \"<string>\" # required","description":"WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't satisfy\nthe spread constraint.\n- DoNotSchedule (default) tells the scheduler not to schedule it.\n- ScheduleAnyway tells the scheduler to schedule the pod in any location,\n  but giving higher precedence to topologies that would help reduce the\n  skew.\nA constraint is considered \"Unsatisfiable\" for an incoming pod\nif and only if every possible node assignment for that pod would violate\n\"MaxSkew\" on some topology.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 3/1/1:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n| P P P |   P   |   P   |\nIf WhenUnsatisfiable is set to DoNotSchedule, incoming pod can only be scheduled\nto zone2(zone3) to become 3/2/1(3/1/2) as ActualSkew(2-1) on zone2(zone3) satisfies\nMaxSkew(1). In other words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but scheduler\nwon't make it *more* imbalanced.\nIt's a required field.","depth":5,"field":"whenUnsatisfiable","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4691,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4691"},{"index":4692,"text":"          # LabelSelector is used to find matching pods. Pods that match this","description":"LabelSelector is used to find matching pods. Pods that match this","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector"},{"index":4693,"text":"          # label selector are counted to determine the number of pods in their","description":"label selector are counted to determine the number of pods in their","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector"},{"index":4694,"text":"          # corresponding topology domain.","description":"corresponding topology domain.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector"},{"index":4695,"text":"          labelSelector: # optional, mapType: atomic","description":"LabelSelector is used to find matching pods.\nPods that match this label selector are counted to determine the number of pods\nin their corresponding topology domain.","depth":5,"field":"labelSelector","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector"},{"index":4696,"text":"            # matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are ANDed with labelSelector\nto select the group of existing pods over which spreading will be calculated\nfor the incoming pod. The same key is forbidden to exist in both MatchLabelKeys and LabelSelector.\nMatchLabelKeys cannot be set when LabelSelector isn't set.\nKeys that don't exist in the incoming pod labels will\nbe ignored. A null or empty list means only match against labelSelector.\n\nThis is a beta field and requires the MatchLabelKeysInPodTopologySpread feature gate to be enabled (enabled by default).","depth":5,"field":"matchLabelKeys","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].matchLabelKeys","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-matchlabelkeys"},{"index":4735,"text":"            # - \"<string>\"","depth":7,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].matchLabelKeys[]","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-matchlabelkeys"},{"index":4736,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4736"},{"index":4737,"text":"          # MinDomains indicates a minimum number of eligible domains. 0) if new Pod is","description":"scheduled, because computed skew will be 3(3 - 0) if new Pod is","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":4755,"text":"          # scheduled to any of the three zones, it will violate MaxSkew.","description":"scheduled to any of the three zones, it will violate MaxSkew.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":4756,"text":"          # minDomains: <int32>","description":"MinDomains indicates a minimum number of eligible domains.\nWhen the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys is less than minDomains,\nPod Topology Spread treats \"global minimum\" as 0, and then the calculation of Skew is performed.\nAnd when the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys equals or greater than minDomains,\nthis value has no effect on scheduling.\nAs a result, when the number of eligible domains is less than minDomains,\nscheduler won't schedule more than maxSkew Pods to those domains.\nIf value is nil, the constraint behaves as if MinDomains is equal to 1.\nValid values are integers greater than 0.\nWhen value is not nil, WhenUnsatisfiable must be DoNotSchedule.\n\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 2, MinDomains is set to 5 and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/2:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |  P P  |\nThe number of domains is less than 5(MinDomains), so \"global minimum\" is treated as 0.\nIn this situation, new pod with the same labelSelector cannot be scheduled,\nbecause computed skew will be 3(3 - 0) if new Pod is scheduled to any of the three zones,\nit will violate MaxSkew.","depth":5,"field":"minDomains","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":4757,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4757"},{"index":4758,"text":"          # NodeAffinityPolicy indicates how we will treat Pod's","description":"NodeAffinityPolicy indicates how we will treat Pod's","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":4759,"text":"          # nodeAffinity/nodeSelector when calculating pod topology spread skew.","description":"nodeAffinity/nodeSelector when calculating pod topology spread skew.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":4760,"text":"          # Options are: - All nodes are included in the calculations.\n\nIf this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Honor policy.","depth":5,"field":"nodeAffinityPolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":4768,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4768"},{"index":4769,"text":"          # NodeTaintsPolicy indicates how we will treat node taints when","description":"NodeTaintsPolicy indicates how we will treat node taints when","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":4770,"text":"          # calculating pod topology spread skew. optional","description":"ephemeral represents a volume that is handled by a cluster storage driver.\nThe volume's lifecycle is tied to the pod that defines it - it will be created before the pod starts,\nand deleted when the pod is removed.\n\nUse this if:\na) the volume is only needed while the pod runs,\nb) features of normal volumes like restoring from snapshot or capacity\n   tracking are needed,\nc) the storage driver is specified through a storage class, and\nd) the storage driver supports dynamic volume provisioning through\n   a PersistentVolumeClaim (see EphemeralVolumeSource for more\n   information on the connection between this volume type\n   and PersistentVolumeClaim).\n\nUse PersistentVolumeClaim or one of the vendor-specific\nAPIs for volumes that persist for longer than the lifecycle\nof an individual pod.\n\nUse CSI for light-weight local ephemeral volumes if the CSI driver is meant to\nbe used that way - see the documentation of the driver for\nmore information.\n\nA pod can use both types of ephemeral volumes and\npersistent volumes at the same time.","depth":5,"field":"ephemeral","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].ephemeral","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-ephemeral"},{"index":5117,"text":"            # the owner of the PVC, i.e. the PVC will be deleted together with","description":"the owner of the PVC, i.e. the PVC will be deleted together with","depth":6,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate"},{"index":5120,"text":"            # Required, must not be nil.","description":"Required, must not be nil.","depth":6,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate"},{"index":5137,"text":"            volumeClaimTemplate: # optional","description":"Will be used to create a stand-alone PVC to provision the volume.\nThe pod in which this EphemeralVolumeSource is embedded will be the\nowner of the PVC, i.e. the PVC will be deleted together with the\npod.  The name of the PVC will be `<pod name>-<volume name>` where\n`<volume name>` is the name from the `PodSpec.Volumes` array\nentry. Pod validation will reject the pod if the concatenated name\nis not valid for a PVC (for example, too long).\n\nAn existing PVC with that name that is not owned by the pod\nwill *not* be used for the pod to avoid using an unrelated\nvolume by mistake. Starting the pod is then blocked until\nthe unrelated PVC is removed. If such a pre-created PVC is\nmeant to be used by the pod, the PVC has to updated with an\nowner reference to the pod once the pod exists. Normally\nthis should not be necessary, but it may be useful when\nmanually reconstructing a broken cluster.\n\nThis field is read-only and no changes will be made by Kubernetes\nto the PVC after it has been created.\n\nRequired, must not be nil.","depth":6,"field":"volumeClaimTemplate","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate"},{"index":5138,"text":"              # The specification for the PersistentVolumeClaim. This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non\ncore object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.\nWhen this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of\nthe specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic\nprovisioner.\nThis field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such\nif both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards\ncompatibility, when namespace isn't specified in dataSourceRef,\nboth fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same\nvalue automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.\nWhen namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,\ndataSource isn't set to the same value and must be empty.\nThere are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:\n* While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef\n  allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.\n* While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef\n  preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is\n  specified.\n* While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects\n  in any namespaces.\n(Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\n(Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.","depth":8,"field":"dataSourceRef","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-datasourceref"},{"index":5200,"text":"                  # This has a different purpose than storageClassName,\nit can be changed after the claim is created. An empty string or nil value indicates that no\nVolumeAttributesClass will be applied to the claim. If the claim enters an Infeasible error state,\nthis field can be reset to its previous value (including nil) to cancel the modification.\nIf the resource referred to by volumeAttributesClass does not exist, this PersistentVolumeClaim will be\nset to a Pending state, as reflected by the modifyVolumeStatus field, until such as a resource\nexists.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volume-attributes-classes/","depth":8,"field":"volumeAttributesClassName","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeAttributesClassName","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-volumeattributesclassname"},{"index":5294,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5294"},{"index":5295,"text":"                # volumeMode defines what type of volume is required by the","description":"volumeMode defines what type of volume is required by the","depth":8,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeMode","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-volumemode"},{"index":5296,"text":"                # claim.
