The field","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].image","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-image"},{"index":5500,"text":"          # spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this","description":"spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].image","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-image"},{"index":5501,"text":"          # volume type.","description":"volume type.","depth":5,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].image","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-image"},{"index":5502,"text":"          # image:","description":"image represents an OCI object (a container image or artifact) pulled and mounted on the kubelet's host machine.\nThe volume is resolved at pod startup depending on which PullPolicy value is provided:\n\n- Always: the kubelet always attempts to pull the reference. Container creation will fail If the pull fails.\n- Never: the kubelet never pulls the reference and only uses a local image or artifact. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present.\n- IfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn't already present on disk. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present and the pull fails.\n\nThe volume gets re-resolved if the pod gets deleted and recreated, which means that new remote content will become available on pod recreation.\nA failure to resolve or pull the image during pod startup will block containers from starting and may add significant latency. Failures will be retried using normal volume backoff and will be reported on the pod reason and message.\nThe types of objects that may be mounted by this volume are defined by the container runtime implementation on a host machine and at minimum must include all valid types supported by the container image field.\nThe OCI object gets mounted in a single directory (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.mountPath) by merging the manifest layers in the same way as for container images.\nThe volume will be mounted read-only (ro) and non-executable files (noexec).\nSub path mounts for containers are not supported (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.subpath) before 1.33.\nThe field spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this volume type.","depth":5,"field":"image","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].image","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-image"},{"index":5503,"text":"            # Policy for pulling OCI objects. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present.\nIfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn't already present on disk. Esoteric PEM features such as inter-block\ncomments and block headers are stripped.  Certificates are deduplicated.\nThe ordering of certificates within the file is arbitrary, and Kubelet\nmay change the order over time.","depth":8,"field":"clusterTrustBundle","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle"},{"index":5676,"text":"                  # Relative path from the volume root to write the bundle.","description":"Relative path from the volume root to write the bundle.","depth":9,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.path","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-path"},{"index":5677,"text":"                  path: \"<string>\" # required","description":"Relative path from the volume root to write the bundle.","depth":9,"field":"path","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.path","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-path"},{"index":5678,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5678"},{"index":5679,"text":"                  # Select all ClusterTrustBundles that match this label","description":"Select all ClusterTrustBundles that match this label","depth":9,"path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-labelselector"},{"index":5680,"text":"                  # selector. Once the signer approves the\nrequest and issues a certificate chain, Kubelet writes the key and\ncertificate chain to the pod filesystem.  The pod does not start until\ncertificates have been issued for each podCertificate projected volume\nsource in its spec.\n\nKubelet will begin trying to rotate the certificate at the time indicated\nby the signer using the PodCertificateRequest.Status.BeginRefreshAt\ntimestamp.\n\nKubelet can write a single file, indicated by the credentialBundlePath\nfield, or separate files, indicated by the keyPath and\ncertificateChainPath fields.\n\nThe credential bundle is a single file in PEM format.  The first PEM\nentry is the private key (in PKCS#8 format), and the remaining PEM\nentries are the certificate chain issued by the signer (typically,\nsigners will return their certificate chain in leaf-to-root order).\n\nPrefer using the credential bundle format, since your application code\ncan read it atomically.  If you use keyPath and certificateChainPath,\nyour application must make two separate file reads. If these coincide\nwith a certificate rotation, it is possible that the private key and leaf\ncertificate you read may not correspond to each other.  Your application\nwill need to check for this condition, and re-read until they are\nconsistent.\n\nThe named signer controls chooses the format of the certificate it\nissues; consult the signer implementation's documentation to learn how to\nuse the certificates it issues.","depth":8,"field":"podCertificate","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate"},{"index":5854,"text":"                  # The maximum allowable\nvalue is 7862400 (91 days).\n\nThe signer implementation is then free to issue a certificate with any\nlifetime *shorter* than MaxExpirationSeconds, but no shorter than 3600\nseconds (1 hour). If not present, default values are used."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-eppconfig-config-saturationdetector-kvcacheutilthreshold","path":"spec.eppConfig.config.saturationDetector.kvCacheUtilThreshold","type":"number","required":false,"description":"KVCacheUtilThreshold defines the KV cache utilization (0.0 to 1.0) above\nwhich a pod is considered to have insufficient capacity."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-eppconfig-config-saturationdetector-metricsstalenessthreshold","path":"spec.eppConfig.config.saturationDetector.metricsStalenessThreshold","type":"string","required":false,"description":"MetricsStalenessThreshold defines how old a pod's metrics can be.\nIf a pod's metrics are older than this, it might be excluded from\n\"good capacity\" considerations or treated as having no capacity for\nsafety."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-eppconfig-config-saturationdetector-queuedepththreshold","path":"spec.eppConfig.config.saturationDetector.queueDepthThreshold","type":"integer","required":false,"description":"QueueDepthThreshold defines the backend waiting queue size above which a\npod is considered to have insufficient capacity for new requests."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-eppconfig-config-schedulingprofiles","path":"spec.eppConfig.config.schedulingProfiles","type":"array<object>","required":true,"description":"SchedulingProfiles is the list of named SchedulingProfiles\nthat will be created."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-eppconfig-config-schedulingprofiles-name","path":"spec.eppConfig.config.schedulingProfiles[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name specifies the name of this SchedulingProfile"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-eppconfig-config-schedulingprofiles-plugins","path":"spec.eppConfig.config.schedulingProfiles[].plugins","type":"array<object>","required":true,"description":"Plugins is the list of plugins for this SchedulingProfile. Fields here\nare explicitly NOT covered by the normal v1beta1 deprecation policy;\ndo not depend on them for production workloads."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint","type":"object","required":false,"description":"checkpoint configures container-image snapshotting and restore for\nthis component. When set, the DGD controller can produce a\nDynamoCheckpoint CR from a running pod and later restore pods from\nthat checkpoint for faster cold start. The user-facing shape of this\nfield -- especially its interaction with the standalone\nDynamoCheckpoint resource and the identity-hash computation -- is\nstill settling, which is why it lives under `experimental` in v1beta1\ninstead of at the top level.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-validations[0].rule: (has(self.checkpointRef) && size(self.checkpointRef) > 0) || has(self.identity)","x-kubernetes-validations[0].message: When checkpoint is configured, either checkpointRef or identity must be specified","x-kubernetes-validations[1].rule: !has(self.job) || !has(self.checkpointRef) || size(self.checkpointRef) == 0","x-kubernetes-validations[1].message: checkpoint.job cannot be set when checkpointRef is specified","x-kubernetes-validations[2].rule: !has(self.job) || !has(self.mode) || self.mode == 'Auto'","x-kubernetes-validations[2].message: checkpoint.job can only be set in Auto mode"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-checkpointref","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.checkpointRef","type":"string","required":false,"description":"checkpointRef references an existing DynamoCheckpoint CR by `metadata.name`.\nWhen set, this component's `identity` is ignored and the referenced\ncheckpoint is used directly."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity","type":"object","required":false,"description":"identity defines the checkpoint identity for hash computation. Used\nwhen `mode` is `Auto` or when looking up existing checkpoints.\nRequired when `checkpointRef` is not specified.","metadata":["requiredFields: backendFramework, model"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-backendframework","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.backendFramework","type":"string","required":true,"description":"backendFramework is the runtime framework (`vllm`, `sglang`, `trtllm`).","metadata":["enum: \"vllm\" | \"sglang\" | \"trtllm\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-dtype","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.dtype","type":"string","required":false,"description":"dtype is the data type (`fp16`, `bf16`, `fp8`, etc.)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-dynamoversion","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.dynamoVersion","type":"string","required":false,"description":"dynamoVersion is the Dynamo platform version. If empty, the version is\nnot included in the identity hash, so checkpoints remain compatible\nacross releases."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-extraparameters","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.extraParameters","type":"object","required":false,"description":"extraParameters are additional parameters that affect the checkpoint hash."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-extraparameters-key","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.extraParameters.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-maxmodellen","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.maxModelLen","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"maxModelLen identifier (e.g. \"meta-llama/Llama-3-70B\").","metadata":["minLength: 1"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-pipelineparallelsize","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.pipelineParallelSize","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"pipelineParallelSize is the pipeline parallel configuration.","metadata":["default: 1","format: int32","minimum: 1"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-identity-tensorparallelsize","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.identity.tensorParallelSize","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"tensorParallelSize is the tensor parallel configuration.","metadata":["default: 1","format: int32","minimum: 1"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-job","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.job","type":"object","required":false,"description":"job customizes the checkpoint Job that is created in Auto mode."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-checkpoint-job-gmsclientcontainers","path":"spec.experimental.checkpoint.job.gmsClientContainers","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"gmsClientContainers Must match\n`spec.experimental.gpuMemoryService.mode` (or\n`spec.components[*].experimental.gpuMemoryService.mode` inside a\nDynamoGraphDeployment).","metadata":["default: \"IntraPod\"","enum: \"IntraPod\" | \"InterPod\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-failover-numshadows","path":"spec.experimental.failover.numShadows","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"numShadows is the number of shadow (standby) engine containers per\nrank. Reserved for future use; the operator currently creates exactly\none shadow.","metadata":["default: 1","format: int32","minimum: 1","maximum: 1"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-gpumemoryservice","path":"spec.experimental.gpuMemoryService","type":"object","required":false,"description":"gpuMemoryService configures the GPU Memory Service (GMS). In each rendered pod, only\nmatching container names are wired; absent names are ignored.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: set"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-gpumemoryservice-extraclientcontainers","path":"spec.experimental.gpuMemoryService.extraClientContainers[]","type":"string","required":true,"metadata":["minLength: 1","maxLength: 63","pattern: ^[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?$"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-gpumemoryservice-extraclientpods","path":"spec.experimental.gpuMemoryService.extraClientPods","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"extraClientPods declares additional GMS client pods for inter-pod GMS. This field is\nreserved for future use and is rejected until inter-pod client orchestration is wired.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-gpumemoryservice-extraclientpods-name","path":"spec.experimental.gpuMemoryService.extraClientPods[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"name identifies this client pod.","metadata":["minLength: 1","maxLength: 63","pattern: ^[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?$"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-gpumemoryservice-extraclientpods-podtemplate","path":"spec.experimental.gpuMemoryService.extraClientPods[].podTemplate","type":"object","required":true,"description":"podTemplate configures the pod to run as a GMS client.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-preserve-unknown-fields"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-experimental-gpumemoryservice-mode","path":"spec.experimental.gpuMemoryService.mode","type":"string","required":false,"description":"mode selects the GMS deployment topology.","metadata":["default: \"IntraPod\"","enum: \"IntraPod\" | \"InterPod\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-frontendsidecar","path":"spec.frontendSidecar","type":"string","required":false,"description":"frontendSidecar optionally designates a container in\n`podTemplate.spec.containers` as the frontend sidecar. The value must\nmatch the `name` of a container in that list; the operator merges its\nfrontend-sidecar defaults (auto-generated Dynamo env vars, ports,\nhealth probes) into that container the same way it merges into `\"main\"`.\nThe full container definition (image, args, envFrom, env) lives in\n`podTemplate` -- this eliminates the redundant `image`, `args`,\n`envFromSecret`, and `envs` fields from v1alpha1's `FrontendSidecarSpec`.\nThe validation webhook rejects values that do not match any container\nname in `podTemplate.spec.containers`."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-globaldynamonamespace","path":"spec.globalDynamoNamespace","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"globalDynamoNamespace places the component in the global Dynamo\nnamespace rather than the per-deployment namespace derived from the\nDGD name."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-modelref","path":"spec.modelRef","type":"object","required":false,"description":"modelRef references a model served by this component. When specified,\na headless service is created for endpoint discovery.","metadata":["requiredFields: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-modelref-name","path":"spec.modelRef.name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"name is the base model identifier (e.g. \"llama-3-70b-instruct-v1\").","metadata":["minLength: 1"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-modelref-revision","path":"spec.modelRef.revision","type":"string","required":false,"description":"revision is the model revision/version."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-multinode","path":"spec.multinode","type":"object","required":false,"description":"multinode configures multinode components."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-multinode-nodecount","path":"spec.multinode.nodeCount","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"nodeCount is the number of nodes to deploy for the multinode component.\nTotal GPUs used is `nodeCount * container GPU request`.","metadata":["default: 2","format: int32","minimum: 2"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-name","path":"spec.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"name is the stable logical identifier for this component within its\nDynamoGraphDeployment. It must be unique within the parent's\n`spec.components` list.\n\nFor standalone DynamoComponentDeployment objects, the defaulting webhook\npopulates `name` from `metadata.name` on admission, so users\ntypically do not need to set it explicitly.\n\n`name` is decoupled from the underlying Kubernetes resource name so that\nthe operator can rename child workloads (e.g. suffixing worker DCDs with\na hash during rolling updates) without losing the stable identity that\ndownstream consumers (labels, status maps, DGDSA references, planner\nRBAC, EPP filters) depend on.","metadata":["minLength: 1","maxLength: 63","pattern: ^[A-Za-z0-9]([-A-Za-z0-9]*[A-Za-z0-9])?$"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate","path":"spec.podTemplate","type":"object","required":false,"description":"podTemplate is the pod template used to create the component's pods.\nThe operator injects its defaults (image, command, env, ports, probes,\nresources, volume mounts) into the container named `\"main\"` inside\n`podTemplate.spec.containers`, merging user overrides by name. If no\ncontainer named `\"main\"` is present, the operator auto-generates it\nwith standard defaults. All other containers in `podTemplate.spec.containers`\nare treated as user-managed sidecars: the operator does not inject\ndefaults into them, so sidecars must specify required fields (e.g. `image`)\nthemselves. The validation webhook rejects pod templates where a\nnon-`\"main\"` container is missing a required field such as `image`."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-metadata","path":"spec.podTemplate.metadata","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Standard object's metadata.\nMore info: containers"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-activedeadlineseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.activeDeadlineSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional integer.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity","type":"object","required":false,"description":"If specified, the pod's scheduling node affinity scheduling rules for the pod."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference","type":"object","required":true,"description":"A node selector term, associated with the corresponding weight.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference-matchexpressions","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference.matchExpressions","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"A list of node selector requirements by node's labels.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference-matchexpressions-key","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference.matchExpressions[].key","type":"string","required":true,"description":"The label key that the selector applies to."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference-matchexpressions-operator","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference.matchExpressions[].operator","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Represents a key's relationship to a set of values.\nValid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. Gt, and Lt."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference-matchexpressions-values","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference.matchExpressions[].values","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"An array of string values. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm","type":"object","required":true,"description":"Required. The requirements are ANDed."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-labelselector-matchlabels-key","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.labelSelector.matchLabels.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.\nThe same key is forbidden to exist in both matchLabelKeys and labelSelector.\nAlso, matchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn't set.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-mismatchlabelkeys","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.mismatchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MismatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe anti-affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling anti-affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and subtracting\n\"weight\" from the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm","type":"object","required":true,"description":"Required. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-lifecycle-prestop-exec","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the\ncommand  is root ('/') in the container's filesystem. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-livenessprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-name","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name of the container specified as a DNS_LABEL.\nEach container in a pod must have a unique name (DNS_LABEL).\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-ports","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].ports","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"List of ports to expose from the container. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-readinessprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resizepolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resizePolicy","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Resources resize policy for the container.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resizepolicy-resourcename","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resizePolicy[].resourceName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name of the resource to which this resource resize policy applies.\nSupported values: cpu, memory."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resizepolicy-restartpolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resizePolicy[].restartPolicy","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Restart policy to apply when specified resource is resized.\nIf not specified, it defaults to NotRequired."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Compute Resources required by this container.\nCannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources-claims","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources.claims","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims,\nthat are used by this container.\n\nThis field depends on the\nDynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.\n\nThis field is immutable. It can only be set for containers.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources-claims-name","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources.claims[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name must match the name of one entry in pod.spec.resourceClaims of\nthe Pod where this field is used. It makes that resource available\ninside a container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources-claims-request","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources.claims[].request","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Request is the name chosen for a request in the referenced claim.\nIf empty, everything from the claim is made available, otherwise\nonly the result of this request."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources-limits","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources.limits","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources-limits-key","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources.limits.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources-requests","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources.requests","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.\nIf Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,\notherwise to an implementation-defined value. Requests cannot exceed Limits.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-resources-requests-key","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].resources.requests.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"RestartPolicy defines the restart behavior of individual containers in a pod.\nThis overrides the pod-level restart policy. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the\ncontainer should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Constraints on the rules:\n- At most 20 rules are allowed.\n- Rules can have the same action.\n- Identical rules are not forbidden in validations.\nWhen rules are specified, container MUST set RestartPolicy explicitly\neven it if matches the Pod's RestartPolicy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-action","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].action","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the requirements\nare satisfied. The only possible value is \"Restart\" to restart the\ncontainer."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Represents the exit codes to check on container exits.","metadata":["requiredFields: operator"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-operator","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.operator","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Represents the relationship between the container exit code(s) and the\nspecified values. Possible values are:\n- In: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is in the\n  set of specified values.\n- NotIn: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is\n  not in the set of specified values."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values","type":"array<integer/int32>","required":false,"description":"Specifies the set of values to check for container exit codes.\nAt most 255 elements are allowed.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: set"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values[]","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext","type":"object","required":false,"description":"SecurityContext defines the security options the container should be run with.\nIf set, the fields of SecurityContext override the equivalent fields of PodSecurityContext.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-allowprivilegeescalation","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"AllowPrivilegeEscalation controls whether a process can gain more\nprivileges than its parent process. This bool directly controls if\nthe no_new_privs flag will be set on the container process.\nAllowPrivilegeEscalation is true always when the container is:\n1) run as Privileged\n2) has CAP_SYS_ADMIN\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"appArmorProfile is the AppArmor options to use by this container. If set, this profile\noverrides the pod's appArmorProfile.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile loaded on the node that should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust match the loaded name of the profile.\nMust be set if and only if type is \"Localhost\"."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-type","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of AppArmor profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n  Localhost - a profile pre-loaded on the node.\n  RuntimeDefault - the container runtime's default profile.\n  Unconfined - no AppArmor enforcement."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-capabilities","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.capabilities","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-capabilities-add","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.capabilities.add","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Added represent POSIX capabilities type"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-capabilities-drop","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Removed represent POSIX capabilities type"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-privileged","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.privileged","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Run container in privileged mode.\nProcesses in privileged containers are essentially equivalent to root on the host.\nDefaults to false.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-procmount","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.procMount","type":"string","required":false,"description":"procMount denotes the type of proc mount to use for the containers.\nThe default value is Default which uses the container runtime defaults for\nreadonly paths and masked paths.\nThis requires the ProcMountType feature flag to be enabled.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-readonlyrootfilesystem","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container has a read-only root filesystem.\nDefault is false.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-runasgroup","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.runAsGroup","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The GID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nUses runtime default if unset.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-runasnonroot","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.runAsNonRoot","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Indicates that the container must run as a non-root user.\nIf true, the Kubelet will validate the image at runtime to ensure that it\ndoes not run as UID 0 (root) and fail to start the container if it does.\nIf unset or false, no such validation will be performed.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-runasuser","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.runAsUser","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The SELinux context to be applied to the container.\nIf unspecified, the container runtime will allocate a random SELinux context for each\ncontainer.  May also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-level","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.level","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Level is SELinux level label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-role","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.role","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Role is a SELinux role label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-type","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.type","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Type is a SELinux type label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-user","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.user","type":"string","required":false,"description":"User is a SELinux user label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-seccompprofile","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seccompProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The seccomp options to use by this container. If seccomp options are\nprovided at both the pod & container level, the container options\noverride the pod options.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-seccompprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seccompProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust be a descending path, relative to the kubelet's configured seccomp profile location.\nMust be set if type is \"Localhost\". Must NOT be set for any other type."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-seccompprofile-type","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.seccompProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of seccomp profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n\nLocalhost - a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nRuntimeDefault - the container runtime default profile should be used.\nUnconfined - no profile should be applied."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The Windows specific settings applied to all containers.\nIf unspecified, the options from the PodSecurityContext will be used.\nIf set in both SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is linux."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspec","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpec","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpec is where the GMSA admission webhook\n(https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/windows-gmsa) inlines the contents of the\nGMSA credential spec named by the GMSACredentialSpecName field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspecname","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpecName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpecName is the name of the GMSA credential spec to use."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-hostprocess","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"HostProcess determines if a container should be run as a 'Host Process' container.\nAll of a Pod's containers must have the same effective HostProcess value\n(it is not allowed to have a mix of HostProcess containers and non-HostProcess containers).\nIn addition, if HostProcess is true then HostNetwork must also be set to true."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-runasusername","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"The UserName in Windows to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to the user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext. If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe","type":"object","required":false,"description":"StartupProbe indicates that the Pod has successfully initialized.\nIf specified, no other probes are executed until this completes successfully.\nIf this probe fails, the Pod will be restarted, just as if the livenessProbe failed.\nThis can be used to provide different probe parameters at the beginning of a Pod's lifecycle,\nwhen it might take a long time to load data or warm a cache, than during steady-state operation.\nThis cannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-exec","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.exec","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.exec.command","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the\ncommand  is root ('/') in the container's filesystem. The command is simply exec'd, it is\nnot run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions ('|', etc) won't work. To use\na shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.\nExit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.exec.command[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-failurethreshold","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.failureThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.\nDefaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-grpc","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.grpc","type":"object","required":false,"description":"GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-grpc-port","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.grpc.port","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-grpc-service","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.grpc.service","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest\n(see https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md).\n\nIf this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-httpget","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.httpGet","type":"object","required":false,"description":"HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-httpget-host","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.httpGet.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-tcpsocket","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket","type":"object","required":false,"description":"TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-host","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-port","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Number or name of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-startupprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].startupProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-stdin","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].stdin","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container should allocate a buffer for stdin in the container runtime. If this\nis not set, reads from stdin in the container will always result in EOF.\nDefault is false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-stdinonce","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].stdinOnce","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether the container runtime should close the stdin channel after it has been opened by\na single attach. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-terminationmessagepath","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].terminationMessagePath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's termination message\nwill be written is mounted into the container's filesystem.\nMessage written is intended to be brief final status, such as an assertion failure message.\nWill be truncated by the node if greater than 4096 bytes. The total message length across\nall containers will be limited to 12kb.\nDefaults to /dev/termination-log.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-terminationmessagepolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].terminationMessagePolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Indicate how the termination message should be populated. File will use the contents of\nterminationMessagePath to populate the container status message on both success and failure.\nFallbackToLogsOnError will use the last chunk of container log output if the termination\nmessage file is empty and the container exited with an error.\nThe log output is limited to 2048 bytes or 80 lines, whichever is smaller.\nDefaults to File.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-tty","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].tty","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container within the container at which the volume should be mounted.  Must\nnot contain ':'."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-volumemounts-mountpropagation","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].mountPropagation","type":"string","required":false,"description":"mountPropagation determines how mounts are propagated from the host\nto container and the other way around.\nWhen not set, MountPropagationNone is used.\nThis field is beta in 1.10.\nWhen RecursiveReadOnly is set to IfPossible or to Enabled, MountPropagation must be None or unspecified\n(which defaults to None)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-volumemounts-name","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"This must match the Name of a Volume."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-volumemounts-readonly","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Mounted read-only if true, read-write otherwise (false or unspecified).\nDefaults to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","type":"string","required":false,"description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-containers-volumemounts-subpath","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Path path within the volume from which the container's volume should be mounted.\nBehaves similarly to SubPath but environment variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-livenessprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-name","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name The command is simply exec'd, it is\nnot run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions ('|', etc) won't work. To use\na shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.\nExit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.exec.command[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-failurethreshold","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.failureThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.\nDefaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-grpc","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.grpc","type":"object","required":false,"description":"GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-grpc-port","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.grpc.port","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"Port number of the gRPC service. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-tcpsocket","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.tcpSocket","type":"object","required":false,"description":"TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-tcpsocket-host","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-tcpsocket-port","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Number or name of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-resizepolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].resizePolicy","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Resources resize policy for the container.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-resizepolicy-resourcename","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].resizePolicy[].resourceName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name of the resource to which this resource resize policy applies.\nSupported values: cpu, memory."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-resizepolicy-restartpolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].resizePolicy[].restartPolicy","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Restart policy to apply when specified resource is resized.\nIf not specified, it defaults to NotRequired."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-resources","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].resources","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Resources are not allowed for ephemeral containers. Requests cannot exceed Limits.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-resources-requests-key","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].resources.requests.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-restartpolicy","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].restartPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Restart policy for the container to manage the restart behavior of each\ncontainer within a pod.\nYou cannot set this field on ephemeral containers."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-restartpolicyrules","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].restartPolicyRules","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the\ncontainer should be restarted on exit. If set, this profile\noverrides the pod's appArmorProfile.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile loaded on the node that should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust match the loaded name of the profile.\nMust be set if and only if type is \"Localhost\"."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1beta1-spec-podtemplate-spec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-type","path":"spec.podTemplate.spec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type
